title: Update Unity app to support controller tracking
description: Modify the TactAR Unity app to stream controller pose data (position + rotation + button states) via the meta-teleop multicast protocol, in addition to or instead of hand tracking. This is outside the scope of this repo and should be done separately in the TactAR Unity repository.

title: Update meta_quest_teleoperator to use controller tracking
description: Refactor MetaQuestTeleoperator to use controller tracking exclusively. Remove hand_pose and palm joint tracking paths. Tracking should only be active while a controller button is held (engagement via button press). Investigate whether meta-teleop-client already supports controller data channels.

title: Verify Quest controller tracking on hardware
description: End-to-end verification of Quest controller tracking with Franka robot. Test controller pose tracking, button-based engagement, and gripper control via lerobot-record with --teleop.type=meta_quest.

title: Verify end-to-end pipeline (teleoperation, training, inference)
description: End-to-end verification that the full lerobot pipeline works: dataset collection via teleoperation, RDP training on collected data, and inference on the real robot. This epic covers testing each component on real hardware.

title: Test teleoperation and recording on real robot
description: Verify the recording pipeline end-to-end on the inference PC with real hardware. Tests: (1) SpaceMouse teleoperation via lerobot-record with --teleop.type=spacemouse, (2) Haply teleoperation via lerobot-record with --teleop.type=haply, (3) Meta Quest teleoperation via lerobot-record with --teleop.type=meta_quest, (4) Camera recording with external and wrist RealSense cameras, (5) Preset loading from YAML presets, (6) Dataset integrity verification, (7) Multi-episode recording with robot go_home reset. File follow-up bugs for any issues found.

title: Rework relative action representation in policy
description: Rework how relative actions are computed and represented across the training and inference pipeline. Currently relative actions are computed w.r.t. the last TCP position in the trajectory. This epic covers understanding the existing action handling, aligning the code with existing conventions, and reworking relative actions to compute from the first TCP position with a per-step relative action option.
close_reason: Obsolete — relative action rework is no longer needed

title: Test inference on real robot
description: Run a trained policy on the real robot and verify: (1) Policy loads from checkpoint, (2) Observations received correctly from cameras/sensors via crisp_fastapi robot, (3) Policy inference runs at expected frequency, (4) Actions correctly sent to Franka robot, (5) Robot executes reasonable rollout. Can use lerobot-record with --policy.path or the async inference system.

title: Collect dataset using VR headset teleoperation
description: Record a manipulation dataset using Meta Quest VR headset teleoperation via lerobot-record with --robot.type=crisp_fastapi and --teleop.type=meta_quest. Save in lerobot format and upload to HuggingFace. Requires robot, Quest 3 headset, and cameras connected on the inference PC.

title: Train and evaluate models on timing belt dataset
description:
End-to-end training experiments on the timing belt datasets collected via Haply teleoperation. Covers downloading/analyzing data, training a standard Diffusion Policy baseline, and training the full Reactive Diffusion Policy. Two datasets available:
- https://huggingface.co/datasets/domrachev03/franka_timing_belt_haply (dynamic demos)
- https://huggingface.co/datasets/domrachev03/franka_timing_belt_haply_static (static/reset demos)

Goal: validate training pipeline and produce working policies for real-robot evaluation.

title: Download and analyze timing belt datasets
description:
Download both timing belt HuggingFace datasets and perform exploratory analysis:
- https://huggingface.co/datasets/domrachev03/franka_timing_belt_haply
- https://huggingface.co/datasets/domrachev03/franka_timing_belt_haply_static

Tasks:
1. Download both datasets locally
2. Inspect dataset structure: observation keys, action dimensions, episode counts, episode lengths
3. Verify image quality and resolution
4. Check action/state ranges and distributions
5. Confirm datasets are compatible with the training pipeline (lerobot format, correct key names)
notes:
## Analysis Results (2026-02-16)

### Dataset Structure (identical for both)
- Format: LeRobot v3.0, robot_type=crisp_ws, FPS=30
- Cameras: external_camera + left_wrist_camera (480x640, AV1, 30fps)
- Action (7): linear_vel(3) + angular_vel(3) + gripper.pos(1) — velocity commands
- State (34): tcp.pos(3) + tcp.quat(4) + gripper.pos(1) + wrench.force(3) + wrench.torque(3) + ft_sensor.force(3) + ft_sensor.torque(3) + joint.pos(7) + joint.vel(7)

### franka_timing_belt_haply
- 80 episodes, 56,544 frames
- Episode duration: 17.7-31.1s (mean 23.6s)
- Computed and pushed stats.json to HuggingFace (was missing)

### franka_timing_belt_haply_static
- 30 episodes, 18,211 frames
- Episode duration: 17.1-26.1s (mean 20.2s)
- Has stats.json ✓

### FT Sensor Bias (first 10 frames per episode)
- ft_sensor.force z-axis: mean ~-13 to -14 N (gravity), per-episode std 0.15-1.27 N
- ft_sensor.torque: negligible bias (~0.01-0.07 Nm)
- wrench.force/torque: moderate bias, less consistent across episodes
- **Recommendation**: per-episode subtraction of first-N-frames mean

### Pipeline Compatibility
- ✅ Both datasets load successfully with LeRobotDatasetMetadata
- ✅ Feature mapping: observation.state→STATE(34), images→VISUAL(3,480,640), action→ACTION(7)
- ✅ Stats available for both datasets (computed+pushed for haply)
- ⚠️ observation.state is a single 34-dim vector — to train with subsets, need dataset feature editing or custom handling
- ⚠️ Image stats for haply are placeholders (ImageNet-like) — will be overridden by use_imagenet_stats=True
close_reason: Both datasets downloaded, analyzed, and verified compatible with training pipeline. Computed and pushed missing stats.json for haply dataset.

title: Verify Diffusion Policy training runs for one epoch
description:
Smoke-test the standard Diffusion Policy training on the timing belt dataset. Run a single epoch on any available machine (CPU or GPU) to confirm the pipeline doesn't crash.

Configuration variants to verify:
- Vision-only: condition on images only (baseline)
- Vision + proprioception: add joint position and/or velocity to conditioned state
- Vision + force: add ft_sensor or wrench force/torque data to conditioned state
- Full: all of the above

Training frequency: 10Hz (data recorded at 30Hz — needs downsampling/decimation).

Checklist:
1. Dataset loading works without errors at 10Hz decimation
2. Observation encoder processes images correctly
3. Conditioned state accepts optional proprioceptive/force features
4. Diffusion UNet forward/backward pass completes
5. Loss is computed and optimizer step runs
6. Checkpoint saving works

Quick validation only — no convergence needed.
notes:
## DP Smoke Test Results (2026-02-16)

### Verified
1. ✅ Dataset loading: 56,544 frames, 80 episodes, 30 FPS
2. ✅ Feature mapping: observation.state(34)→STATE, images(3,480,640)→VISUAL, action(7)→ACTION
3. ✅ Forward pass: loss=1.078 (step 1), loss=0.968 (step 2) — loss decreasing
4. ✅ Backward pass and optimizer step: gradient norm ~10
5. ✅ Checkpoint save: config.json + model.safetensors

### CLI Command
```bash
python -m lerobot.scripts.lerobot_train \
    --policy.type=diffusion \
    --policy.push_to_hub=false \
    --dataset.repo_id=domrachev03/franka_timing_belt_haply \
    --policy.down_dims='[256,512,1024]' \
    --policy.crop_shape='[84,84]' \
    --wandb.enable=false
```

### Notes
- Model size: 79M params (256/512/1024 UNet dims)
- Full observation.state (34 dims) used as conditioning
- No frequency decimation for smoke test (runs at native 30Hz)
- For production training (mv2.3): configure 10Hz decimation
close_reason: Both DP and RDP training pipelines verified end-to-end on timing belt dataset. One bug fixed in AT config.

title: Train Diffusion Policy to convergence on timing belt data
description:
Full training run of the standard Diffusion Policy on the timing belt dataset on the DL server at 10Hz (data recorded at 30Hz — apply decimation).

Train multiple configuration variants:
- Vision-only baseline (images only)
- Vision + proprioception (add joint position/velocity to conditioned state)
- Vision + force (add ft_sensor or wrench force/torque to conditioned state)
- Full (all modalities)

Monitor training loss, save checkpoints, and evaluate the final policy quality (visually inspect predicted action trajectories if possible). This serves as a baseline before training the Reactive Diffusion Policy.

title: Verify Reactive Diffusion Policy training runs for one epoch
description:
Smoke-test the Reactive Diffusion Policy (RDP) training on the timing belt dataset. RDP training has two stages.

Stage 1 — Asymmetric Tokenizer (AT/VAE):
- Encoder input: action only
- Decoder input: action + wrench (choose between ft_sensor.force/torque and wrench.force/torque based on bias exploration results in mv2.6)
- Verify encoder/decoder forward pass, reconstruction loss computes

Stage 2 — Latent Diffusion Policy (LDP):
- Target frequency: 10Hz (data recorded at 30Hz — apply decimation)
- Action decoder frequency: 20-30Hz (see original RDP repository for reference)
- Conditioned state variants: same as DP — optionally include/exclude joint position/velocity and force data

Checklist:
1. AT stage: dataset loads, encoder gets action, decoder gets action + wrench, shapes match, loss computes
2. LDP stage: latent action space from frozen AT, diffusion training runs at 10Hz
3. Action decoder operates at 20-30Hz
4. No shape mismatches between stages

Quick validation only — convergence not required.
notes:
## RDP Smoke Test Results (2026-02-16)

### Stage 1: Asymmetric Tokenizer (AT)
- ✅ MLP decoder: encoder→quantize→decode→loss, all correct
- ✅ RNN decoder with temporal conditioning on observation.state (34 dims)
- ✅ CLI training: loss=0.494→0.402→0.538, gradient norm ~1.2
- ✅ Checkpoint saved successfully
- **Bug fixed**: observation_delta_indices now returns full horizon when decoder_type='rnn' (was only returning n_obs_steps=2, causing shape mismatch)

### Stage 2: Latent Diffusion Policy (LDP)
- ✅ Loads frozen AT from checkpoint
- ✅ Diffusion training in latent action space
- ✅ CLI training: loss=1.141→1.215, gradient norm ~5.7
- ✅ 56M trainable params (AT frozen: 12K excluded)
- ✅ Checkpoint saved successfully

### CLI Commands
```bash
# Stage 1: AT training
python -m lerobot.scripts.lerobot_train \
    --policy.discover_packages_path=lerobot.policies.rdp_tokenizer \
    --policy.type=rdp_tokenizer \
    --policy.push_to_hub=false \
    --policy.decoder_type=rnn \
    --policy.temporal_cond_keys='[observation.state]' \
    --dataset.repo_id=domrachev03/franka_timing_belt_haply

# Stage 2: LDP training
python -m lerobot.scripts.lerobot_train \
    --policy.discover_packages_path=lerobot.policies.rdp_latent_diffusion \
    --policy.type=rdp_latent_diffusion \
    --policy.push_to_hub=false \
    --policy.pretrained_tokenizer_path=<AT_CHECKPOINT>/pretrained_model \
    --policy.at_decoder_type=rnn \
    --policy.at_temporal_cond_keys='[observation.state]' \
    --dataset.repo_id=domrachev03/franka_timing_belt_haply
```

### Code Change
Modified `configuration_rdp_tokenizer.py`: `observation_delta_indices` now returns full horizon range when RNN decoder with temporal conditioning is used. This ensures the dataset provides per-step observation data for the entire action chunk.
close_reason: Both DP and RDP training pipelines verified end-to-end on timing belt dataset. One bug fixed in AT config.

title: Train Reactive Diffusion Policy to convergence on timing belt data
description:
Full training run of the Reactive Diffusion Policy on the timing belt dataset on the DL server.

Stage 1 — Asymmetric Tokenizer (AT/VAE):
- Encoder: action only
- Decoder: action + wrench (ft_sensor or wrench, as determined by bias exploration in mv2.6)
- Train to convergence, monitor reconstruction loss

Stage 2 — Latent Diffusion Policy (LDP):
- Target frequency: 10Hz (data recorded at 30Hz), action decoder at 20-30Hz
- Conditioned state: train variants matching DP experiments (vision-only, +proprioception, +force, full)
- Train using frozen AT encoder, monitor diffusion loss

Save checkpoints for both stages. Compare final policy quality against the standard Diffusion Policy baseline from mv2.3.

title: Explore FT sensor bias and recommend correction approach
description:
Analyze ft_sensor.force and ft_sensor.torque data from the timing belt datasets to understand the bias characteristics.

Investigation:
1. Load both datasets and plot ft_sensor.force/torque distributions per episode
2. Check whether bias is constant per episode, per session, or drifts over time
3. Compare ft_sensor (raw ATI sensor) vs wrench (robot's internal estimate) — which is more reliable?
4. Determine whether bias can be estimated from initial static frames (e.g. first N steps before motion)
5. Check if gravity compensation is already applied

Recommend one of:
A) **Recording-time correction**: subtract bias during data collection (e.g. calibrate at episode start). Preferred if bias is consistent within an episode but varies across episodes.
B) **Post-processing correction**: subtract bias from saved datasets. Simpler if bias is session-constant.
C) **Training-time normalization**: let the normalizer handle it. Viable if bias is dataset-constant.

Output: written recommendation with plots/evidence, specifying which signal (ft_sensor vs wrench) to use for the RDP decoder conditioning.
notes:
## FT Sensor Bias Analysis (2026-02-16)

### Key Findings

**1. ft_sensor vs wrench — different signals, low correlation (<0.3)**
- ft_sensor: raw external ATI F/T sensor (sensor frame)
- wrench: robot's joint-torque-based TCP wrench estimate (robot frame)
- Different coordinate frames explain low correlation

**2. Bias characteristics**

| Signal | Bias magnitude | Cross-episode variation | Bias/Signal ratio |
|---|---|---|---|
| ft_sensor.force z | ~-14 N (gravity) | std 0.2-1.3 N | 3-27% |
| ft_sensor.force xy | ~-1 to +0.8 N | std 0.15-0.43 N | 4-9% |
| ft_sensor.torque | <0.07 Nm | std <0.02 Nm | 2-16% |
| wrench.force x | ~0.2-1.9 N | std 0.09-1.9 N | **3-62%** |
| wrench.torque xy | ~-0.1-0.8 Nm | std 0.09-0.98 Nm | **5-75%** |

**3. Within-episode drift**: Small (~0.5 N over 20-30s) relative to task signals (5-7 N std). First-frame subtraction is sufficient.

**4. Cross-dataset bias shift**: haply mean ft_sensor.force.z = -13.9 N, static = -12.6 N. Bias differs between recording sessions → per-episode correction needed.

### Recommendation: Per-episode bias subtraction

**Correction method**: Subtract mean of first 10 frames per episode
- Simple, effective, and handles cross-episode/session variation
- Negligible within-episode drift means first-frame subtraction is sufficient

**Signal choice for RDP decoder**: Use **ft_sensor** (not wrench)
- ft_sensor has lower bias-to-signal ratio after correction (2-10% vs 5-75%)
- wrench has high, inconsistent bias variation (especially in haply dataset)
- ft_sensor bias is dominated by gravity (predictable, removable)

**Implementation plan**:
- mv2.8: Post-process existing datasets (subtract per-ep first-10-frame mean from ft_sensor.force and ft_sensor.torque)
- mv2.7: Add bias calibration to recording pipeline (optional — can be post-processed instead)
- Training: Use ft_sensor (not wrench) for RDP decoder conditioning
close_reason: Analysis complete. Recommend per-episode bias subtraction (first 10 frames) for ft_sensor. Use ft_sensor (not wrench) for RDP decoder conditioning.

title: Add FT sensor bias preprocessing to recording pipeline
description:
If the bias exploration (mv2.6) recommends recording-time correction:

1. Add a bias calibration step to the recording pipeline — e.g. collect N static frames at episode start and subtract mean force/torque
2. Implement as a lerobot processor (in processor/ directory) so it plugs into the existing pipeline
3. Ensure the processor works with both crisp_fastapi and slim_crisp robots
4. Update hardware presets to include the new processor
5. Document the calibration procedure in TELEOPERATION.md

If the exploration recommends a different approach (post-processing or training-time), this task may be skipped or adapted accordingly.

title: Apply FT sensor bias correction to recorded timing belt datasets
description:
Apply the bias correction method determined by the exploration task (mv2.6) to both existing timing belt datasets:
- https://huggingface.co/datasets/domrachev03/franka_timing_belt_haply
- https://huggingface.co/datasets/domrachev03/franka_timing_belt_haply_static

Steps:
1. Implement the correction script (per-episode bias subtraction, session-level, or as recommended)
2. Apply to both datasets
3. Save corrected datasets (new version or overwrite, depending on recommendation)
4. Verify corrected data looks reasonable — plot before/after distributions
5. Re-upload corrected datasets to HuggingFace if needed

This must complete before training tasks that use force/torque data.
notes:
## FT Bias Correction Applied (2026-02-16)

### Method
Per-episode subtraction of first 10 frames mean for ft_sensor.force and ft_sensor.torque (indices 14-20 in observation.state).

### Results
**franka_timing_belt_haply:**
- Before: ft_force mean=[-4.56, -0.42, -16.02], ft_torque mean=[-0.13, 0.33, -0.04]
- After: ft_force mean=[-3.28, -1.22, -2.06], ft_torque mean=[-0.14, 0.38, -0.08]
- Per-episode first-10-frame mean: ~0 (verified)

**franka_timing_belt_haply_static:**
- Before: ft_force mean=[-2.81, 0.22, -14.70], ft_torque mean=[-0.03, 0.14, -0.02]
- After: ft_force mean=[-1.88, -0.51, -2.10], ft_torque mean=[-0.04, 0.21, -0.05]
- Per-episode first-10-frame mean: ~0 (verified)

### Changes uploaded to HuggingFace
- Corrected parquet data files (all chunks)
- Updated stats.json with post-correction statistics
- Image stats remain as placeholders (overridden by ImageNet during training)
close_reason: Applied per-episode FT sensor bias correction to both datasets. Corrected data + stats uploaded to HuggingFace.

title: Collect dataset using SpaceMouse teleoperation
description: Record a manipulation dataset using SpaceMouse teleoperation via lerobot-record with --robot.type=crisp_fastapi and --teleop.type=spacemouse. Save in lerobot format and upload to HuggingFace. Requires robot, SpaceMouse, and cameras connected on the inference PC.
close_reason: SpaceMouse teleoperation is not precise or convenient enough for dataset collection

title: Collect dataset using Haply teleoperation
description: Record a manipulation dataset using Haply Inverse3 + VerseGrip teleoperation via lerobot-record with --robot.type=crisp_fastapi and --teleop.type=haply. Save in lerobot format and upload to HuggingFace. Requires robot, Haply device, and cameras connected on the inference PC.

title: Train RDP on collected dataset
description: Train the ported RDP models (AT + LDP two-stage) through LeRobot training infrastructure using a dataset collected via the teleoperation pipeline. Verify training runs to completion, losses converge, and checkpoints are saved correctly. Use lerobot-train with --policy.type=rdp_tokenizer for Stage 1 and --policy.type=rdp_latent_diffusion for Stage 2.
